evals.name: dark-portfolio
evals.prompt: Build me a minimal dark portfolio site for a product designer. I want it to feel premium and refined, like Linear or Vercel's site. Focus on clean typography and generous white space. The designer's name is Mika Chen, they specialize in SaaS product design.
evals.expected_output: A Design Decision Document with specific hex codes, font names, pixel values, followed by ordered forge CLI command sequences for each section (nav, hero, projects grid, about, contact, footer).

assertions.text: DDD contains actual hex color codes (#XXXXXX format)
DDD names specific font families (e.g. Space Grotesk, DM Sans), not generic 'sans-serif'
DDD has specific pixel values for spacing and font sizes
DDD specifies at most 2 typefaces (display + body) — a monospace font for code/terminal is allowed as exception
All spacing values in DDD are multiples of 8 (or 4 for icon padding)
Build plan contains forge CLI commands (forge insert, forge style, etc.)
Build plan includes forge screenshot commands after major sections
Colors and fonts in forge commands match the DDD values

evals.name: saas-landing
evals.prompt: Create a landing page for 'DevForge', a developer CLI tool that automates code reviews. Inspired by Linear and Raycast. Dark mode, technical audience. Needs hero with product screenshot area, features grid (3 columns), social proof logos section, pricing table (3 tiers), and footer. The tagline is 'Code reviews on autopilot.'
evals.expected_output: A DDD with dark palette, developer-oriented typography, followed by section-by-section forge CLI build plan with specific style values matching the DDD.

assertions.text: DDD contains actual hex color codes (#XXXXXX format)
DDD names specific font families, not generic 'sans-serif'
DDD has specific pixel values for spacing and font sizes
DDD specifies at most 2 typefaces (display + body) — a monospace font for code/terminal is allowed as exception
All spacing values in DDD are multiples of 8
Build plan contains forge CLI commands
Build plan includes forge screenshot after sections
Build plan covers all 5 requested sections: hero, features grid, social proof, pricing, footer

evals.name: vague-brief
evals.expected_output: The skill should ask 2-3 clarifying questions about site type, aesthetic direction, audience, and content before proceeding. It should NOT generate a DDD or start building.

assertions.text: Response contains clarifying questions (question marks present)
Does NOT produce a full DDD without direction
Asks about site type or purpose
Asks about aesthetic direction, style, or references

evals.name: grade-screenshot
evals.prompt: Review this design for me. It's a dark-mode SaaS landing page with: a hero section using 64px Space Grotesk headline on #0A0A0A background with #E8E8E8 text, 18px DM Sans subhead in #888888, a #4F46E5 CTA button with 12px border-radius. Below is a features grid with 3 cards at 32px padding, #141414 background, 16px border-radius, 1px border at rgba(255,255,255,0.06). Section spacing is 96px. All spacing is on 8px grid. Contrast ratios all pass AA.
evals.expected_output: A Visual QA Report grading this as A or high B across all dimensions, with the anti-slop checklist passing, and only minor polish suggestions if any.

assertions.text: Report includes separate grades for typography, color, spacing, layout, and polish
Overall grade is A or B (the described design follows best practices)
Report includes anti-slop checklist

evals.name: diagnose-issues
evals.prompt: Evaluate this design: a landing page with 3 different fonts (Inter, Playfair Display, and Fira Code used for body text), cards with mixed border-radius (8px on some, 16px on others, 24px on the CTA), #FFFFFF text on #1A1A1A background, section spacing of 45px, button padding of 11px, and a purple-to-pink gradient hero background. The body text is 13px.
evals.expected_output: A Visual QA Report grading this as D or F, identifying: 3 fonts (max 2), mixed border-radius, spacing not on 8px grid (45px, 11px), text below 14px (13px), pure white text in dark mode, and the purple-to-pink gradient anti-pattern.

assertions.text: Flags the 3-font violation (Inter + Playfair Display + Fira Code)
Flags inconsistent border-radius (8px/16px/24px mixed)
Flags 13px body text as below 14px minimum
Flags purple-to-pink gradient as anti-pattern
Flags 45px or 11px spacing as not on 8px grid
Overall grade is D or F
Provides specific fix recommendations

evals.name: anti-slop-detection
evals.prompt: I built a hero section with a purple-to-pink gradient background (#8B5CF6 to #EC4899), Inter font at 400 weight on a muted gray palette, centered text spanning the full 1440px container width, and a stock photo of people in an office. The heading is 42px and the body text is 12px. What do you think?
evals.expected_output: Should flag multiple anti-slop violations: purple-to-pink gradient (#1), Inter on timid palette (#2), centered text on wide container (#5), stock photo (#6), and text below 14px (#4). Should recommend specific alternatives.

assertions.text: Identifies purple-to-pink gradient as anti-pattern
Flags Inter at 400 weight on muted palette as generic/timid
Flags centered text spanning full 1440px as too wide for center alignment
Flags stock photo of people in office
Flags 12px body text as below 14px minimum
Suggests specific alternative choices (different gradient, font, alignment)
